channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Voters in Gabon await results of presidential election, with likely victory for coup leader
item.description: Voters in Gabon are waiting for the results of the central African nation’s presidential election, which the country’s military rulers hope will legitimize their grip on power.
item.dc:creator: Yves Laurent Goma And Chinedu Asadu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: Across Europe and further afield, counterterrorism agencies are grappling with a new generation of young people who are being radicalized online and consuming ultraviolent content produced by extremist groups and their supporters.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ecuador's incumbent president and a leftist lawyer again vie for the top job in a runoff election
item.description: A conservative young millionaire and a leftist lawyer are again the choices before Ecuadorians heading to the polls for the country’s second presidential runoff election in less than two years.
item.dc:creator: Gonzalo Solano And Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New gear could keep California crab fishermen on the water longer, and whales safe
item.description: After years of a shortened crab fishing season aimed at preventing whale entanglements off the West Coast, California crabbers are experimenting with a new fishing method that allows them to stay on the water longer while keeping the marine mammals safe.
item.dc:creator: Amy Taxin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of failing to pause strikes after US envoy leaves Moscow
item.description: Russia and Ukraine’s top diplomats on Saturday used a high-level conference in Turkey to once again trade accusations of violating a tentative U.S.-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges of negotiating an end to the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump team holds 'constructive' face-to-face nuclear talks with Iran, will meet again next weekend
item.description: The U.S. and Iran held rare face-to-face nuclear talks in Oman Saturday. The Trump administration is pushing for diplomacy backed by military pressure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Serbia's populist president demands order after months of anti-corruption protests shaking his rule
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic on Saturday demanded that authorities restore “order and peace” in the Balkan country following months of anti-corruption protests that have shaken his firm grip on power.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attacks on famine-hit camps in Sudan's Darfur leave at least 100 people dead, UN official says
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan's notorious paramilitary group launched a two-day attack on famine-hit camps for displaced people that left more than 100 dead, including 20 children and nine aid workers, in the Darfur region, a U.N. official said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With Trump's backing Israel pushes deeper into Gaza as pressure builds for hostage deal
item.description: An Israeli official says military operations and hostage talks are fully coordinated with the U.S., as officials remain optimistic about a deal. Israel continues its advance in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico to send water to Texas amid Trump tariff threats
item.description: Mexico will facilitate an “immediate” delivery of water to farmers in Texas to help shore up resources amid President Trump’s tariff threats, Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: High winds and sandstorms prompt Beijing to cancel hundreds of flights and close parks
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — High winds and the threat of sandstorms Saturday prompted Beijing to cancel hundreds of flights and close public parks, as massive gales felled hundreds of trees, crushing cars and damaging older homes across China’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delegates from Iran, US holding talks in Oman amid ongoing tensions: What to know
item.description: Delegates from the United States and Iran are holding talks in Oman Saturday in a delicate effort to restart negotiations over Tehran’s controversial nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN humanitarian agency to cut staff by 20% due to 'brutal cuts' in funding
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. humanitarian agency said it is cutting its 2,600 staff who operate in more than 60 countries by 20% because of “brutal cuts” in funding that have left it with a nearly $60 million shortfall. U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-China tariff battle fuels questions about Trump's endgame
item.description: President Trump's escalating tariff battle with China is rattling the global economy and raising questions about how the standoff will end.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, The Hill

item.title: Serbian capital braces for a mega pro-president rally as tensions surge in the Balkan country
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Chanting patriotic songs, thousands of supporters of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic gathered in downtown Belgrade on Friday, a day ahead of what authorities expect to be a huge rally in his support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European countries vow billions in military support for Ukraine as US envoy meets Putin
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European countries vowed Friday to sends billions of dollars in further funding to help Ukraine keep fighting Russia’s invasion, as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Harry makes visit to Ukraine, meets with wounded veterans
item.description: Prince Harry is touching down in Ukraine, getting a first-hand look at a rehabilitation center in the war-torn country. The Duke of Sussex visited the Superhumans Center in Lviv on Thursday, a spokesperson confirmed to ITK.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: In a turbulent time, Turkey lays claim to a role of stability for Europe, Syria and global trade
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Friday sought to position his country as a point of stability in an increasingly tumultuous world, saying Turkey was essential for European security and able to span geopolitical divisions over Ukraine, Syria and U.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of children have been raped and sexually abused in eastern Congo, UNICEF says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Thousands of children have been subjected to rape and sexual violence in conflict-battered eastern Congo over the course of two months, the U.N. children's agency said Friday, warning that existing funding gaps meant that hundreds of thousands were deprived of protection.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's Xi tells Spanish PM that partnership needed, no mention of talks with US
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China calls on Western countries to work to support multilateralism and open cooperation, Chinese President Xi Jinping told Spain’s visiting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez on Friday, as Beijing woos allies for its escalating tariff fight with U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian opposition leader says his home was the target of an alleged bomb plot
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian opposition leader Peter Dutton confirmed on Friday that his family home had been the target of an alleged bomb plot, but said concerns for his personal safety did not restrict his election campaigning.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Senate approves Peter Hoekstra as next US ambassador to Canada
item.description: The Senate approved Peter Hoekstra to be the next U.S. ambassador to Canada amid one of the toughest times in Washington's relationship with Ottawa as the tariff war heats up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Slain Hamas hostage's family fights for the release of those still in Gaza
item.description: Oded Lifshitz, an 83-year-old great-grandfather, returned to Israel in a coffin more than 500 days after he was taken from his home. Now, his family is fighting for the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Stellacci, WFLA, NBC
